SALOON.Definition: a bar where people drank alcohol

BROTHEL.Definition: a place where people paid for sex

SURRENDER.Definition: the act of stopping fighting and giving up

ICONIC.Definition: very famous and easy to recognise

GLINTING.Definition: shining with small flashes of light

OUTLAWS.Definition: criminals who lived outside the law

EXPANDING WESTWARD.Definition: moving and growing towards the west

PROPERTY REGISTRIES.Definition: official records showing who owned land or buildings

FREE-FOR-ALL.Definition: a situation with no rules or control

DRIFTERS.Definition: people who moved from place to place without staying long

LAWLESSNESS.Definition: a situation where laws were not obeyed

SYSTEMATICALLY SLAUGHTERED.Definition: killed in a planned and organised way

STAGECOACHES.Definition: large horse-drawn vehicles for carrying people or mail

EXECUTION.Definition: the act of killing someone as a legal punishment

SKIPPED TOWN.Definition: left the place quickly to avoid trouble

DEPUTY MARSHAL.Definition: a law officer who helped a marshal

PORTFOLIO CAREER.Definition: a career with many different jobs

PROSTITUTION.Definition: the work of having sex for money

APPOINT.Definition: officially choose someone for the job

NEPOTISM.Definition: giving jobs to family members unfairly

RESERVED.Definition: quiet and not very open with people

LOOSE NETWORK.Definition: a group connected in a weak or informal way

SMUGGLERS.Definition: people who secretly moved illegal goods

RUNNING FEUD.Definition: a long and continuing fight or argument

RANCHING.Definition: raising cattle or other animals on large farms

WARINESS.Definition: a feeling of not fully trusting them

OUTSIDERS.Definition: people who did not belong to the place

SIMMERING.Definition: continuing quietly and slowly getting worse

CITY LIMITS.Definition: the official borders of the town

GUNFIGHTER.Definition: a person known for fighting with guns

JUSTIFIED.Definition: shown to be right or reasonable

LAW ENFORCERS.Definition: people who made sure laws were obeyed

SETTLE A PRIVATE SCORE.Definition: get revenge for a personal problem

TIT-FOR-TAT.Definition: an action done in revenge for another action

VENDETTA.Definition: a long and bitter fight between the groups

WARRANT.Definition: an official paper allowing police to arrest him

PROPERTY SPECULATOR.Definition: a person who bought land or buildings hoping to sell later for more money

LAND BOOM.Definition: a time when land prices rose very quickly

CONTROVERSIALLY.Definition: in a way that caused disagreement

HOUSEHOLD NAMES.Definition: very famous people known by almost everyone

DIME NOVELS.Definition: cheap popular books with exciting stories

MATCH-FIXING.Definition: secretly changing the result of a sports event unfairly

MANUSCRIPT.Definition: a piece of writing before it was published

DEFINITIVE.Definition: seen as the best and most complete

FIX HIS REPUTATION.Definition: improve what people thought about him

PIMPING.Definition: controlling prostitution and making money from it

EMBODIMENT.Definition: a person that clearly showed or represented them

RETELLING.Definition: a new version of telling the story

FIREARMS ORDINANCE.Definition: a local law about carrying guns

CHEQUERED PAST.Definition: a past full of problems or bad behaviour

CLEARING HIS NAME.Definition: proving he was not guilty or dishonest
